skill.name: linear-automation
skill.description: Automate Linear issues, projects, cycles, labels, and comments through the Rube MCP Linear toolkit.
skill.summary: Run Linear issue, project, cycle, label, comment, and GraphQL workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static shell-execution, weak-cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown tool names, parameter names, and words such as description. The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated: the skill routes OAuth-backed Linear actions through an external Rube MCP endpoint and includes a custom GraphQL tool that can query or mutate workspace data.

medium_findings.title: OAuth-Backed Linear Mutation Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to use Rube MCP with an active Linear connection and then create or update Linear issues, projects, labels, and comments. This is expected functionality, but it can modify production project data if used without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly require an active Linear connection and list create or update tools. This confirms legitimate mutation capability, but no malicious instructions or covert behavior were found.

medium_findings.title: Custom Linear GraphQL Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill includes LINEAR_RUN_QUERY_OR_MUTATION for advanced GraphQL operations. This can be useful, but arbitrary query or mutation strings increase the chance of unintended data exposure or workspace changes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill directly documents a GraphQL query or mutation tool and identifies the query and variables inputs. The risk is contextual because execution still depends on the authorized Linear connection.

low_findings.title: External MCP Endpoint Dependency
low_findings.description: The setup instructions direct users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This is an expected network dependency for the skill, but users should understand that Linear operations are mediated by a third-party MCP service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint is explicitly documented and matches the stated Rube MCP integration. It is not evidence of exfiltration, but it is a real third-party network dependency.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Pattern Findings
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown inline code around tool names, not executable commands. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections are false positives from words and parameter names such as description, team_id, issue_id, and tool reference rows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and the cited content is tool names, parameters, or workflow steps. No executable script, shell interpolation, cryptographic implementation, or host reconnaissance command was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Service Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires configuring an external MCP server at rube.app for Linear automation. This should be disclosed because the agent will rely on that service to reach Linear.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly present in setup instructions. The pattern is a disclosed dependency, not hidden network behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace Mutation Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow lists tools that create and update Linear issues, projects, labels, and comments. These actions should require clear user intent and workspace context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequence includes several create and update operations. The behavior is transparent and aligns with the skill purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Custom GraphQL Query Or Mutation Tool
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill exposes a workflow for custom Linear GraphQL query or mutation strings. Review generated GraphQL before execution, especially when mutations are requested.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The GraphQL workflow is clearly documented with query and variables parameters. The risk depends on the requested operation and Linear permissions.

content.user_title: Automate Linear Workflows with Rube MCP
content.value_statement: Linear teams lose time repeating issue, project, cycle, label, and comment updates. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows that resolve IDs, use current schemas, and perform Linear actions.
actual_capabilities: Checks Rube MCP availability and Linear connection status before workflows.
Finds Linear team, state, cycle, issue, project, label, and user IDs through documented tool sequences.
Creates, searches, retrieves, and updates Linear issues with team, state, assignee, priority, and label fields.
Creates and updates Linear projects, labels, and issue comments through Rube MCP Linear tools.
Runs custom Linear GraphQL queries or mutations when standard tools do not cover the requested operation.
Documents Linear-specific pitfalls such as team scoping, state IDs, priority integers, and pagination cursors.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Linear OAuth connection.
Does not include standalone scripts, tests, or local automation code.
Cannot guarantee current Linear schemas without calling RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first.
Custom GraphQL use requires user review and knowledge of Linear permissions.

use_cases.title: Create Team Issues
use_cases.description: Turn product requests into Linear issues with the right team, state, priority, labels, and assignee.

use_cases.title: Maintain Delivery Projects
use_cases.description: Create or update Linear projects and comments while keeping team IDs and project states consistent.

use_cases.title: Query Complex Workspace Data
use_cases.description: Use custom GraphQL when standard Linear tools cannot answer a workflow, reporting, or migration question.

prompt_templates.title: Check Linear Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Linear automation skill to confirm Rube MCP is available, search for current Linear tools, and check whether my Linear connection is active.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Linear Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Linear issue for the Platform team titled [title]. Use priority [priority], state [state], labels [labels], and this description: [description]. Confirm the resolved IDs before creating it.

prompt_templates.title: Update Project Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Linear project named [project name], review its current state, and update it to [new state] with this project note: [note]. Ask before applying the update.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Reviewed GraphQL Operation
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Linear GraphQL operation for [goal]. Show the query, variables, expected effect, and required permissions before running LINEAR_RUN_QUERY_OR_MUTATION.

output_examples.input: Create a bug for the Mobile team about failed biometric login and set priority high.
output_examples.output: The assistant resolves the Mobile team ID, checks workflow states, maps high priority to Linear priority 2, asks for confirmation, and creates the issue.

output_examples.input: Move the Billing project to started and add a short status comment.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the project, verifies the current state, prepares the update and comment, then applies both after user approval.

output_examples.input: Find issues in the current cycle that have no assignee.
output_examples.output: The assistant resolves the team and cycle, lists matching issues with pagination, and summarizes unassigned work by issue title and identifier.

best_practices: Call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS before each workflow so tool schemas are current.
Resolve and show team, state, project, issue, and label IDs before making changes.
Ask for confirmation before create, update, comment, or GraphQL mutation operations.
anti_patterns: Do not assume Linear state names or priority strings match API values.
Do not run custom GraphQL mutations without showing the operation and expected effect first.
Do not create issues, labels, projects, or comments without a clear target team or workspace context.

faq.question: Does this skill require Linear API keys?
faq.answer: No. It expects Rube MCP to manage the Linear connection through OAuth.

faq.question: Can it create and update Linear issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents workflows for issue creation, search, retrieval, and updates.

faq.question: Does it support projects and cycles?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes project create and update flows, plus cycle lookup by team.

faq.question: Why must tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first reduces schema mismatch and parameter errors.

faq.question: Is custom GraphQL safe to use?
faq.answer: It can be safe when reviewed first. Users should inspect queries, variables, permissions, and mutation effects before execution.

faq.question: What are the main risks?
faq.answer: The main risks are third-party MCP routing, OAuth-scoped Linear access, and accidental workspace changes.
